mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment trends upward steadily, equity investors infer ongoing economic expansion and strong corporate revenue prospects, which produces positive average S&P 500 20-day forward returns regardless of short-term noise in other macro indicators.
failure_condition: Over a 10-year backtest, episodes with a 6-month rolling increase in PAYEMS in the top tercile do not show statistically higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than episodes in the bottom tercile (t-test p > 0.05), or the information coefficient between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns remains within ±0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6% that is still falling from prior peaks signals slack being absorbed without immediate overheating, which increases expected corporate earnings growth and reduces recession risk, generating positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 6% and falls over the prior 3 months, the S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases or mean 60-day returns are not higher than unconditional mean at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2% and stable or declining over a 3-month window, discount rates applied to equities remain low and liquidity conditions stay easy, leading to above-average positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Across all instances where FEDFUNDS is below 2% with a nonpositive 3-month change, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly higher than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of positive returns is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above its long-run median indicates broad-based demand strength that supports revenue growth and risk appetite, so when GDPC1 annualized growth is in its top tercile, the S&P 500 tends to earn positive 60-day forward returns despite short-run volatility.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 4-quarter annualized growth in top tercile of its historical distribution
failure_condition: During quarters classified in the top tercile of GDPC1 4-quarter annualized growth, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly higher than in the bottom tercile (t-test p > 0.05) or the sign correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding while the unemployment rate is low and stable, markets interpret this as an overheating late-cycle environment that raises margin pressure and policy tightening risk, leading to below-average S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 1% and UNRATE < 5% with |3-month change in UNRATE| < 0.2pp
failure_condition: During episodes where INDPRO grows more than 1% over 3 months and UNRATE is below 5% with minimal 3-month change, the S&P 500 does not show significantly lower 20-day returns relative to other periods (t-test p > 0.05) or the conditional average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts from already depressed levels signals a deepening housing downturn that weakens construction employment and collateral values, which increases recession probability and generates negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level in bottom quartile of its historical distribution and 6-month change in HOUST < -10%
failure_condition: In instances where HOUST is in the bottom quartile of its history and falls more than 10% over 6 months, the S&P 500 does not exhibit negative average 60-day returns or the share of positive 60-day returns exceeds 50% with no significant underperformance versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains low and unchanged, investors expect an imminent tightening shift and higher real discount rates, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns once this configuration appears.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation above 3% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change between -0.1pp and 0.1pp
failure_condition: Across all episodes with 6-month annualized CPI inflation above 3% while the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is within ±0.1pp, the S&P 500 does not show significantly negative or below-baseline 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) and the hit rate of negative returns is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth decelerates while unemployment rises, the combination signals weakening household demand that compresses revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns during such demand scare episodes.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth in RSAFS below 0% and 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.3pp
failure_condition: For periods where 3-month RSAFS growth is negative and UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 does not experience significantly more negative 5-day returns than in other periods (t-test p > 0.05) or the conditional average 5-day return is not below zero.
